preamble: The uvular nasal [ɴ] weakens and devoices to the voiceless uvular fricative [χ], a transformation typically found in final positions or before voiceless sounds.

examples.note: Potential historical weakening of back nasals in final positions or before voiceless consonants.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
